notes: Evals run against this machine's live agent stores. They assume the fixture project /Users/tjboudreaux/Sandbox/example-app has prior Claude + Codex + Factory history (verified 2026-05-29). If that project is archived, repoint the path in evals 1-2 to any project with known multi-agent history.

evals.prompt: Build a ground truth for /Users/tjboudreaux/Sandbox/example-app from all our previous agent conversations.
evals.expected_output: A coverage map is shown first, then .agents/ground-truth.md is written with cited findings, a Contradictions/unverified section, and a provenance index. Raw transcripts are never read into the orchestrator's context.
expectations: The skill ran scripts/collect.py via Bash with --project pointing at the example-app path
A coverage map was presented to the user BEFORE any synthesis, listing per-agent session counts and date ranges
The coverage map surfaced unmatchable history (e.g. codex sessions_without_cwd and factory flat_files_without_cwd counts) rather than hiding it
The orchestrator did NOT open raw .jsonl transcript files with the Read tool; it read only the normalized .md files produced by the collector (or delegated reading to subagents)
The final .agents/ground-truth.md contains a Coverage report section and a Provenance index section
At least some claims in the ground-truth document carry a citation of the form agent + session + date
The document includes a 'Contradictions & unverified claims' section (may be empty but present)

evals.prompt: Catch me up on the project at /Users/tjboudreaux/Sandbox — only that directory, not the subprojects under it.
evals.expected_output: Exact-cwd scoping is used (no --include-subdirs). The coverage and ground truth cover only sessions whose cwd is exactly /Users/tjboudreaux/Sandbox, excluding child projects like example-app.
expectations: collect.py was run WITHOUT the --include-subdirs flag (exact-match default)
The coverage map / ground truth does NOT include sessions belonging to subprojects such as example-app
The skill explained that monorepo/subdirectory mode is available via --include-subdirs but was intentionally not used here

evals.prompt: Build a ground truth for /tmp/rosetta-eval-cold-project (a directory with no prior agent history).
evals.expected_output: The collector runs without error, the coverage map reports zero sessions across all agents gracefully, and the skill states there is no prior agent history instead of fabricating any — while still checking git/docs.
expectations: collect.py completed successfully and reported 0 matched sessions for every agent without crashing
The skill clearly told the user that no prior agent conversations were found for this project rather than inventing decisions or history
The skill still attempted to anchor on git state and any project docs (or noted their absence) before concluding
